programmes.show_description: (2002) Action thriller starring Vin Diesel as Xander Cage: a thrill-seeking extreme sports enthusiast, stuntman and rebellious athlete-turned-reluctant spy. With Samuel L Jackson.
Roosevelt Inn: Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the owner of the Roosevelt Inn in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho is busy cracking jokes and playing dress up. Can Gordon help?
Adventures in Baby-Getting: Marge gets a new car that's perfect for a family of five - but what if she decides she wants a family of six!?
New Kid on the Block: When Homer gets kicked out of an all-you-can-eat seafood restaurant for eating too much, he takes the owner to court.
Mr Plow: Homer impulsively buys a snow plough and sets up in business as Mr Plow. But when Barney declares himself the Plow King, their rivalry spirals out of control.
Lisa's First Word: The family try to get Maggie to say her first word. But Homer's glad that she hasn't spoken yet, because all Lisa and Bart do is talk back to him.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George attempts to bring the grandeur back to a Georgian town house in Cheshire that's been stripped of its original features, and tackles a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Friends, Romans, Accountants: Norm hosts a Roman toga party at Cheers, but when his handsome, womanising boss takes a shine to Diane, Norm's job could be in jeopardy.
Truce or Consequences: Carla and Diane stop bickering when Diane admits that she needs a friend, but Carla can't resist the temptation to take advantage of her vulnerability.
Coach Returns to Action: Coach develops a foolproof technique for wooing women and astonishes everyone with his success, including Sam, who becomes his rival for neighbour Nina.
Blabbermouths: When Robert tells Ray that Amy filled him in on some juicy gossip about him, Ray decides to get even by sharing some nasty secrets of his own.
The Model: A modelling agent tells Robert that he has a unique look and convinces him to pose for headshots with their in-house photographer.
The Mentor: A young man, Sam Gilula, arrives at Frank and Marie's, and everyone is surprised when he and Frank fondly embrace.
They're Playing Our Song: Frasier goes all out when he's inspired to write a new theme tune for his radio show.
Big Crane on Campus: Frasier learns that beauty is only skin deep when he dates the prom queen from his old high school.
Out with Dad: An unlikely chain of events forces Martin to pretend that he's gay on Valentine's Day.
Something About Dr Mary: Frasier's temporary call-screener, 'Dr' Mary (Kim Coles), hijacks his show with her sassy attitude and no-nonsense advice.
Simmer Down: Short tempers, simmering tensions and downright disrespect are on the menu at Chris's troubled home-cooking café in Georgetown, central Texas.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In North Wales, Henry Cole finds an antique cannon in Sue and Josh's storage shed, while Simon O'Brien discovers a croquet set in need of restoration.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt Baker and his family make more vital changes to their farm in the Durham hills. Top of their to-do list is tackling the destruction from the worst storms in living memory.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman shows them five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget. Are any of them perfect?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, friends Richard and Simon raise funds to renovate an abandoned bar. In France, couple Mark and Gary turn an old distillery into a gîte.
The new run continues. Emma Louise and Liv host at their 527-year-old pub in Kent, where spooky, spectral tales and a chaotic bout of drag bingo and karaoke await the B&Bers.
The new run continues. In Sheffield, Lizzy brings the heat with a Mexican-themed night filled with fiesta vibes, a build-your-own burrito bonanza and an unexpected surprise... (Ep1/5)
...the Sun: No stranger to helping others find their perfect place in the sun, Jasmine Harman takes the plunge and transforms a run-down villa into a family home on the Costa del Sol. (Ep1)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Cooking for Less: Emma and Matt's hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?
Fifteen-year-old Meldred is rushed into A&E after being stabbed in the thigh by a stranger while walking to school. Sports commentator Andrew has a mysterious rash on his ankle.

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: The epic adventure reality contest continues. Predator turns on Predator, but will it pay off? One player goes all in on their biggest threat, and a fiery clash sets rivalries alight. (Ep5)
...for Stand Up To Cancer: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aléshé make decorated traybakes and tarte tatin, and recreate childhood toys in biscuits.
Chilling drama from Walter Presents. An abducted teenager re-appears after three years, but the huge back tattoo isn't the only thing about her that's unrecognisable. In Dutch/subs. (Ep1)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Emma's tempted by romance again for the first time since Edwin. Adele's return to Fairley threatens everything. (Ep5/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Emma prepares the perfect family Christmas, but her obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. (Ep6/8)

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out what's next on their demolition list.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at lofty ambitions in Walthamstow in East London, where the Clayton-Fisher family totally transformed their dark and dingy attic.
Mercedes wants to know what Jenson's up to. Warren makes a decision, but is he too late? Friends support Diane and Tony. And Rex experiments with his image for the first time in public.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Endless Slumper: Sam's old baseball teammate Rick hits a run of bad form, so Sam lends him his good luck charm. But as Rick's game improves, Sam's luck changes for the worse.
One for the Book: The bar's visited by an octogenarian who's the only arrival for his WWI squad reunion, and an awkward young man seeking a fun night before becoming a monk.
The Spy Who Came in for a Cold One: Diane incurs the wrath of Sam when she refuses to tolerate a customer who indulges in far-fetched stories about his exploits as a WWII spy.
Golf for It: Robert and Ray end up with the mother of all guilt trips when they decide not to help Marie with a home project so that they can land a prime spot on the golf course.
The Home: Frank and Marie announce that they're moving into a retirement home and sell their house to Robert and Amy.
Not So Fast: Ray and Debra are shocked to discover that Frank and Marie are going to be thrown out of their apartment.
Whine Club: Frasier's hopes of being elected corkmaster of the wine club are dashed when Niles's girlfriend Mel convinces him to run against his brother.
Hot Pursuit: Martin helps Donny to stake out an unfaithful husband, while Frasier and Roz nearly have a romantic encounter at a conference.
Morning Becomes Entertainment: Frasier and Bebe fill in for a week on a morning TV chat show.
To Thine Old Self Be True: When Frasier tries to plan Donny's bachelor party, he winds up handcuffed to a stripper and embarrasses himself in front of Regan.
La Galleria 33 (Part 1/2): Gordon tries to help La Galleria 33, run by sisters Rita and Lisa, who blame the restaurant's failures on everything from tablecloths to the weather.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole find a gramophone and restyle a stowaway bed. Simon accidentally puts his foot in it with a drum, while Henry decides that he's a silver surfer.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: As a blanket of snow falls, the Bakers ensure that all the animals are fed and protected and make safe an old ash tree that's perilously close to the farmhouse.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sue and Tony want a holiday bolthole in the French city of Carcassonne. With a property already on their radar, can any of Craig Rowe's options for their £100k win them over?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, Mother Nature wreaks havoc on Richard and Simon's bar. In Italy, boiler problems leave Lisa and Paul's first booking in disarray.
The new run continues. At Ami and Mark's plush glampsite in Kent, the B&Bers are hailing their huts. But alarm bells are ringing for one pair in their bell tent.
The new run continues. In Sheffield, Sally hopes to impress with a Shanghai menu, but one guest's starter leaves them tongue tied. But can her piano skills strike the right chord? (Ep2/5)
...the Sun: As the enormity of the build sets in, work gets underway to make the place liveable before the family move in. And Jasmine visits a friend for some renovation inspiration. (Ep2)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. The Davies family have a double date with a Labrador and a German Shepherd puppy. And is Pomeranian Fox about to get lucky?
Artist Sarah returns to her roots in the Durham Dales to build a cannabis-based home. But costs go through the roof. Can she get her hempcrete hub of a home over the line?

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: The epic adventure reality contest continues. With five Predators and two Prey, the players face a brutal reset - and a shock decision delivers the biggest upset of the game so far. (Ep6)
What do Britain's sharpest armchair critics think of Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Race Across the World, The Piano, I Kissed a Boy and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen?
